executive_summary: Global systemic risk remains moderate as physical metrics diverge from market and media perceptions. The elevated Taiwan information panic contrasting with negative physical indicators suggests heightened psychological warfare operations rather than immediate kinetic escalation. Moderate supply chain stress and energy fluctuations in the Middle East require ongoing monitoring to prevent sudden localized disruptions.
correlations: Elevated media panic in Taiwan correlates with a high AI bubble index as speculative capital reacts to perceived semiconductor manufacturing vulnerabilities.
